name: prometheus-configuration
description: Set up Prometheus for comprehensive metric collection, storage, and monitoring of infrastructure and applications. Use when implementing metrics collection, setting up monitoring infrastructure, or configuring alerting systems.
capabilities: Generate Prometheus server configuration with scrape targets
Create recording rules for pre-computed metrics
Design alert rules with severity levels and annotations
Set up TLS authentication for scrape endpoints
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a legitimate Prometheus configuration documentation skill. The static scanner flagged code examples within markdown documentation as executable code, which is a false positive. All detected patterns (external commands, network URLs, certificate paths, crypto algorithms) appear in YAML/bash code blocks showing configuration examples - not actual runtime code. No malicious intent detected. Skill is safe for publication.
valueStatement: Set up Prometheus for comprehensive metric collection, alerting, and infrastructure monitoring. This skill provides complete configuration guides for scrape targets, recording rules, and alert definitions.
limitations: Does not install Prometheus binaries or containers
Does not provide managed Prometheus hosting
Requires user to apply configurations to their Prometheus instance

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer Setting Up Monitoring
useCases.description: Configure Prometheus from scratch for a new infrastructure project with proper scrape targets and alerting

useCases.targetUser: Site reliability engineers defining SLOs and alerts
useCases.title: SRE Team Defining Alert Rules
useCases.description: Create comprehensive alert rules for service availability, error rates, and resource utilization

useCases.title: Developer Instrumenting Applications
useCases.description: Learn Prometheus metric naming conventions and scrape configuration for application endpoints

promptTemplates.title: Basic Prometheus Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up Prometheus monitoring for my Kubernetes cluster. Include scrape configuration for node exporters and a basic alert for service availability.

promptTemplates.title: Recording Rules Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create recording rules for common API metrics including request rate, error rate percentage, and P95 latency. Show how to organize them by service.

promptTemplates.title: Alert Rules Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Design alert rules for high CPU usage (threshold 80% for 5 minutes), high memory usage (threshold 85%), and disk space low (threshold 90%). Include proper labels and annotations.

promptTemplates.title: Kubernetes Service Discovery
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure Prometheus to automatically discover services in Kubernetes using annotations. Show relabeling rules to add environment and region labels.

bestPractices: Use consistent metric naming with prefix_unit format (e.g., http_requests_total)
Set appropriate scrape intervals (15-60s typical, 5s for critical metrics)
Create recording rules for frequently queried expensive expressions
antiPatterns: Avoid scraping too frequently (high resource usage)
Do not alert on instantaneous spikes without for duration
Avoid recording rules that duplicate raw metrics

faq.question: What scrape interval should I use?
faq.answer: Default is 15 seconds. Use 15-60s for most metrics. Use 5-10s for critical real-time metrics but this increases storage and compute requirements.

faq.question: How do I secure Prometheus scrape endpoints?
faq.answer: Use TLS configuration in prometheus.yml with ca_file, cert_file, and key_file options. Enable basic auth via authorization headers.

faq.question: What is the difference between recording rules and alerts?
faq.answer: Recording rules pre-compute expensive queries into new metrics for faster dashboard queries. Alerts trigger notifications when conditions are met.

faq.question: How do I discover targets in Kubernetes?
faq.answer: Use kubernetes_sd_configs with roles: pod, service, node, or ingress. Use prometheus.io annotations like prometheus.io/scrape: 'true'.

faq.question: How do I validate my configuration?
faq.answer: Use promtool check config prometheus.yml and promtool check rules rules.yml to validate syntax before reloading Prometheus.

faq.question: What retention period should I use?
faq.answer: Default is 15 days. Adjust based on storage capacity and monitoring requirements. Use Thanos or Cortex for long-term storage beyond 30 days.
